text.title: azure-app-gateway-waf Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-app-gateway-waf as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: App Gateway v2 (since 2025) requires subnet delegation to `Microsoft.Network/applicationGateways`; agents copying older Terraform templates fail at deploy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The 2025 requirement is not in older documentation or examples. The deploy error is clear but confusing if you don't know to look for it: "Subnet '/subnets/appgw' is not delegated."

failure-mode.description: Agents update the Key Vault certificate on renewal but forget the App Gateway Managed Identity needs `get`/`list` permissions on the new certificate version, causing the gateway to fall back to the old cert silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Key Vault access policies are version-scoped for some policy models. After renewal, the new cert version requires explicit permission grants if the policy is not set to cover all versions.

failure-mode.description: Front Door rules engine syntax differs from App Gateway URL Path Map. Agents copy routing rules between them and break routing silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Front Door uses a different DSL for path matching (wildcards, conditions) than App Gateway's `url_path_map` + `path_rule` Terraform blocks. They are not interchangeable.
